statement: NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra (B300) delivers 15 PFLOPS dense NVFP4 per GPU at up to 1400W TDP, ~50% more NVFP4 compute and HBM capacity per chip than the ~1000W Blackwell (B200) at 10 PFLOPS.
why: Generational compute-density gains (more FLOPS and HBM per chip) improve effective throughput per rack and per watt.

impact.mechanism: Higher per-chip NVFP4 compute and HBM capacity raise performance density, improving effective compute-per-watt supply.

evidence.source: NVIDIA Developer technical blog / DGX product pages
evidence.excerpt: Blackwell Ultra provides '50% more NVFP4 compute and 50% more HBM capacity per chip', NVFP4 '1.5x compared to Blackwell GPU'.

confidence.basis: Vendor spec pages and developer blog (secondary tier); relative compute figures without independent perf-per-watt benchmark.
